text:
In a small town called Heartwood, lived two friends, Lily and Max. They spent their days playing hide-and-seek in the woods behind the old abandoned mill. One sunny afternoon, they found a pile of old relics. Among them was an old, rusted bell with cracked copper letters that read "Hope."

It was decided. They would ring it every night before bed. The bell had magically brought luck to their games, always granting them that one-win-before-they're-found.

Their nightly ritual unfolded with predictable schedules. Lily would dash out, ring the bell, leap behind a bush and giggle as Max chased after her. But one evening, as they were about to repeat their dance, something different happened.

The rusted bell danced closer, its chime hanging in the air like a tune. Max reached out, willing it to ring. It hesitated, resonating with an unexpected clarity. Suddenly, the night was alive with sound—a beautiful symphony of night birds,
